title: Sort Your Incoming Mail
summary: Sort incoming mail into categories and identify what needs attention.
learnerPromise: You will be able to sort mail into useful piles and spot which items need action or a response.
whyItMatters: Mail can include bills, important notices, and time-sensitive documents. Sorting it regularly helps you avoid missed deadlines, late fees, and lost paperwork.
realLifeUses: You come home to a pile of mail and need to find the bills
You want to separate junk mail from items worth keeping
You need to track a letter or package that should have arrived
You are helping someone else manage their household mail
materialsNeeded: A flat surface to sort on
A pen to mark dates or notes
result: Mail is sorted into clear categories and time-sensitive items are easy to find.
youWillLearn: How to quickly spot bills, official notices, and personal letters
How to separate junk mail and items you can discard safely
How to flag items that need a response or payment by a deadline
How to store important mail so it does not get lost
miniChallenge: Take a stack of recent mail and sort it into four piles: needs action, keep for records, read later, and discard. Mark deadlines on anything that needs action.
steps: Gather all recent mail and find a flat surface to work on
Open each piece or glance at the front to identify the sender and topic
Create four piles: needs action, keep for records, read later, and discard
For each item that needs action, write the deadline on a sticky note or the envelope
Put the 'needs action' pile where you will see it and file or recycle the rest
completionCriteria: All mail is sorted into clear categories
Any item with a deadline is marked and placed where you will see it
commonProblems: You are not sure if something is important — check for your name, account numbers, or due dates
Junk mail looks official — look for your name or known account; if unsure, keep it briefly and check online
Mail piles up because you forget to sort — try sorting mail the same day you bring it in
tips: Open mail near a trash can so you can discard junk immediately
If you share a mailbox, write initials on each piece so people know what is theirs
Set a weekly time to review the 'keep for records' pile and move old items to a filing spot
